world.title: Individual tourism after the era of templates: why South Africans are increasingly building holidays
world.description: Not long ago, holidays followed a fixed pattern: a resort, a list of excursions, and full predictability. But travel styles are changing, and today travelers from Johannesburg or Cape Town are designing their own routes — around gastronomy, surfing, festivals, or remote work by the ocean.

world.description: The European Commission will set out plans ⁠to cut electricity taxes and coordinate the summer refill of gas storage, as it seeks to cushion the energy fallout from the Iran war.

world.title: Diarmuid Gavin: Leave the lawn alone and tackle these garden jobs instead
world.description: There’s a saying that April showers bring May flowers so, by that reckoning, next month will be an abundance of blossoms. It’s been a mixed month weather-wise but as temperatures improve, there’s plenty of gardening to be done, and the annual sound of lawnmowers across neighbourhoods has started.

business.title: Chery Group to Present Multi-Brand Technology Matrix at 2026 Beijing Auto Show
business.description: Wuhu，Anhui，China, April 22, 2026 -- Chery Group will showcase its latest multi-brand automotive lineup and technological advancements at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show, opening April 24. The group’s international brand matrix—including CHERY, EXEED, iCAUR, OMODA & JAECOO, LEPAS, and LUXEED—will present 15 core models alongside mobility technologies. The event will also host over 3,000 international guests, representing the company’s largest global delegation at a Beijing Auto Show to date.

science.title: Did Life Exist On Mars? NASA's Curiosity Finds Organic Molecules Never Seen Before On Red Planet
science.description: The rover detected seven different organic compounds in a dried-up lakebed near Mars' equator, five of which had never been seen on the planet before.

science.title: Ancient DNA reveals a hidden Neanderthal group frozen in time
science.description: A remarkable genetic breakthrough has uncovered what may be one of the clearest snapshots yet of a Neanderthal “community” living together 100,000 years ago in what is now Poland. The findings reveal that these individuals shared genetic ties with Neanderthals spread across Europe and the Caucasus, hinting at widespread ancient lineages that later disappeared.

science.title: “Baffling” new snake species in Myanmar looks like multiple species at once
science.description: Scientists have uncovered a fascinating new species of pit viper in Myanmar that seems to blur the very definition of what a species is. This snake, now named the Ayeyarwady pit viper, puzzled researchers because it looks like a mix between two known species—sometimes resembling one, sometimes the other, and occasionally something in between. Initially suspected to be a hybrid, genetic analysis revealed it is actually its own distinct species.
